kurtosis_sqi.name: kurtosis_sqi
kurtosis_sqi.desc: Pearson kurtosis (non-Fisher).  Clean PPG segments have kurtosis ~2-4 due to the pulsatile shape; very high values indicate spikes/motion artifact, very low values indicate near-flat / saturated signal.  Manual-mode bounds are a conservative default; recommended to use auto-tune mode on real recordings.

skewness_sqi.name: skewness_sqi
skewness_sqi.desc: Sample skewness with bias correction.  Clean PPG is slightly right-skewed (sharper systolic peaks than diastolic troughs).  |skew| > 2 typically indicates artifact dominating one tail.
skewness_sqi.ref: Heuristic; intended as a fallback for manual mode.

entropy_sqi.name: entropy_sqi
entropy_sqi.desc: Shannon entropy of a 16-bin amplitude histogram, in nats.  A pure tone gives entropy ~ log(2) ~ 0.69; uniform noise gives ~ log(16) ~ 2.77.  Clean PPG typically sits ~1.5-2.5 because amplitude distribution is concentrated around the systolic peak and diastolic trough but not flat.
entropy_sqi.ref: Empirical; calibrate on your own recordings via auto-tune mode for production use.

snr_sqi.name: snr_sqi
snr_sqi.desc: Per-segment SNR in dB, computed as 20*log10(rms / mad) where MAD is the median absolute deviation as a robust noise floor proxy.  This is a crude SNR — not a spectral SNR.  Clean PPG segments typically sit in 5-25 dB; below 3 dB the segment is dominated by noise; above 30 dB suggests an artificially clean (over-filtered) signal.

zero_crossing_sqi.name: zero_crossing_sqi
zero_crossing_sqi.desc: Zero-crossings per sample (after mean-removal).  Clean PPG should cross the mean roughly twice per cardiac cycle, giving a fraction around (2 * HR) / (60 * fs).  At fs=100 Hz, HR=60 bpm: ~2/6000 ~0.0003.  Bounds are looser to accommodate HR range and short-segment variance.
zero_crossing_sqi.ref: Derived from cardiac physiology; calibrate per recording in production.

perfusion_sqi.name: perfusion_sqi
perfusion_sqi.desc: Perfusion index: (AC peak-to-peak) / |DC mean| * 1000.  Only meaningful when the signal retains its DC component (raw photoplethysmogram, not high-passed).  Values below 50 indicate too little pulsatility (poor finger contact, hypoperfusion); values above 5000 indicate either a high-passed signal or a saturated sensor.
perfusion_sqi.ref: Adapted from vital-sqi rule_dict.json (perfusion_sqi p5/p95: 682-1899); widened to accommodate diverse acquisition setups.

peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi.name: peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi
peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi.desc: Raw segment peak-to-peak.  Lower bound rejects nearly-flat segments; the upper bound is intentionally permissive because PPG amplitude is highly sensor-dependent and not a quality indicator in absolute terms.
peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi.ref: Heuristic; use auto-tune mode for meaningful per-recording bounds.
